name: security-audit
description: Security audit: scan for vulnerabilities, analyze, and fix

parameters.name: test_command
parameters.description: Command to run tests after fixes

team.name: auditor
team.role: Security auditor — identifies vulnerabilities, injection vectors, auth issues, data exposure

team.name: fixer
team.role: Security engineer — implements fixes with minimal surface area changes

tasks.title: Security scan of {{scope}}
tasks.description:
Perform a security audit of {{scope}}. Minimum severity: {{severity}}.

Check for:
- Injection vulnerabilities (SQL, XSS, command injection, path traversal)
- Authentication/authorization issues
- Data exposure (secrets in code, excessive logging, unmasked PII)
- Dependency vulnerabilities (check package.json/lock files)
- Unsafe deserialization, eval, or dynamic code execution
- Missing input validation or sanitization
- Insecure defaults or configurations

Write findings to security-report.md with severity, file:line, and remediation steps.

expectations.criteria: Audit is thorough, findings are real (not false positives), each has severity rating and specific remediation steps

tasks.title: Fix security issues
tasks.description:
Based on security-report.md, fix all issues with severity >= {{severity}} in {{scope}}.

Rules:
- Address the root cause, not just symptoms
- Minimal change surface
- Don't break existing functionality
- Add input validation where missing
- All tests must still pass

expectations.criteria: All identified security issues at or above the target severity are properly fixed without introducing regressions
